title: lasianthus verrucosus
intro: Lasianthus verrucosus, also known as Rough-leaved Lasianthus, is a plant species belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae) and is native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves of Lasianthus verrucosus are rich in tannins and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In traditional medicine, the leaves can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and they can also be used in herbal teas, tinctures, and salves to harness their medicinal properties.
